body.title: DickSimnel: load + execute project skill files via ToolLoop
body.result: skill_load() + _build_system_prompt(); sprint-ticket SKILL.md injected into inference system prompt; 5 tests; committed 2f1a741d
body.description:
DickSimnel must be able to run the project's shared skill procedures (/sprint-ticket, /ticket, /sorted, etc.) using OR inference after June 15 when CC.0 is no longer available on the Anthropic Max plan.

The skills are substrate-independent procedures (SKILL.md files). DickSimnel's ToolLoop should load the relevant SKILL.md as procedural context before working a ticket, then follow the steps with OR inference exactly as CC.0 would.

Design:
1. Add skill_load(name) to devices/dicksimnel/device.py: reads ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, returns content
2. In _run_inference(ticket): check ticket tags for a skill hint; load the matching SKILL.md and prepend it to the ToolLoop system prompt
3. Default skill: sprint-ticket (the core development procedure)
4. Fallback: if no SKILL.md found, use the existing inference prompt unchanged

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py (skill_load, _run_inference update), devices/dicksimnel/tool_loop.py (system_prompt injection point if separate)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; skills are read-only flat files; no DB dependency; skill loading is best-effort (missing SKILL.md = warn, continue)

**Scope boundary:** IN — load SKILL.md as procedural context; inject into ToolLoop prompt; tests verify skill content appears in prompt. OUT — modifying skill files, writing new skills, multi-skill chaining, skill versioning

**Completion criteria:** DickSimnel._run_inference with a sprint-tagged ticket includes the sprint-ticket SKILL.md content in its ToolLoop prompt. Test: mock skill_load(); assert prompt contains skill text. Integration: DickSimnel can close an S-size sprint ticket autonomously using the procedure.
